outcome: Participation restriction and quality of life post intervention
source_quote: Table 5. Global Recovery SIS 3.0: Experimental group post-intervention 86.5 (7.1), Control group 56.7 (4.8), p=0.000. Difference baseline/follow-up EG: -49.5 (-56.4/-42.8), CG: -33.0 (-36.8/-29.1). ANOVA F=70.6, p=0.000, eta2=0.633

outcome: Upper limb function post intervention (composite measure)
source_quote: Table 3. FMA-UE Total: Intervention group T1 (post-intervention) 46.3 +/- 16.8 (n=9), Control group T1 47.8 +/- 12.3 (n=9). Change from T0 to T1: Intervention +2.1, Control -0.2. No significant differences between groups in FMA-UE.

outcome: Upper limb function: subgroup analysis based on amount of therapy
source_quote: No subgroup analysis based on amount of therapy was reported in this paper. The study reported overall FMA-UE, BBT, MAL, and SIS scores but did not stratify by therapy amount.

outcome: Upper limb function post intervention (composite measure)
source_quote: Table 2. Fugl-Meyer assessment scale: Experimental group post 87.95 +/- 14.16 (n=22), Control group post 86.00 +/- 15.97 (n=22). Time x Group interaction F=0.123, p=0.728, eta_p2=0.003 (not significant).

outcome: Upper limb function: subgroup analysis based on amount of therapy
source_quote: No subgroup analysis based on amount of therapy was reported in this paper. The study compared experimental (conventional + VR wearable device) vs control (conventional only) groups without stratifying by therapy amount.

outcome: Upper limb function: subgroup analysis based on time since onset of stroke
source_quote: No subgroup analysis based on time since onset of stroke was reported. All participants were acute phase stroke patients (<=1 month from onset). Mean length of stay was 17.73 +/- 5.98 days (experimental) and 16.82 +/- 7.28 days (control).
